- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	"""Tiered reasoning seam — the single abstraction over LLM execution by stakes tier.
2	
3	No engine component talks to an LLM directly; everything routes through :class:`ReasoningSeam`.
4	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's hardened ``reasoning_client`` (ARCHITECTURE.md
5	§1, ADR-0003). Two execution tiers:
6	
7	- ``StakesTier.BULK`` — a local model runtime (loopback-only, e.g. Ollama) for high-volume,
8	  low-stakes work.
9	- ``StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES`` — a frontier model via a **fixed-cost subscription** path
10	  (the ``claude`` CLI / Agent SDK using an OAuth token). No metered API key is ever used:
11	  every metered Anthropic credential is scrubbed from the subprocess environment first.
12	
13	Hardened invariants preserved from the prior platform:
14	
15	- forward-proof credential scrubbing (any ``ANTHROPIC_*KEY``/``*TOKEN`` stripped; the
16	  subscription ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` preserved);
17	- hard per-call timeouts via a bounded worker pool with a saturation guard;
18	- independent retry budgets — exponential backoff for transient errors, long backpressure
19	  for rate limits — and permanent errors never retried;
20	- robust JSON extraction via a balanced-brace walker (not a greedy regex);
21	- loopback enforcement on the local backend (ADR-0003).

281	    opener = closer = ""
282	    depth = 0
283	    in_string = False
284	    escape = False
285	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):

287	            if ch in "{[":

289	                closer = "}" if ch == "{" else "]"

295	            elif ch == "\\":

297	            elif ch == '"':

304	        elif ch == closer:

335	    """Tier: BULK — local model runtime (loopback-enforced).
336	
337	    ``num_predict`` is always set (bounded generation). For JSON, a caller-supplied
338	    ``json_schema`` uses Ollama's schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path); otherwise
339	    ``json_mode`` falls back to loose ``format="json"``. ``seed`` lets a retry force a different
340	    deterministic decode without sharpening temperature.
341	    """
342	    require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
343	    try:
344	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
345	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
346	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
347	            "bulk tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (langchain-ollama) — not installed"
348	        ) from exc
349	
350	    # BULK's INNER bound (#G-003): the sync-tier counterpart to HIGH_STAKES's anyio.fail_after.
351	    # A ThreadPoolExecutor worker blocked in client.invoke() cannot be interrupted by the outer
352	    # _with_timeout, so the HTTP client timeout is what actually frees the slot on a hung backend.
353	    # A bare float sets EVERY httpx phase (connect/read/write/pool) to BULK_TIMEOUT, so a slow
354	    # connect THEN a slow read could total ~2*BULK_TIMEOUT — past the outer BULK_TIMEOUT+10 bound,
355	    # leaking the slot. Use an explicit Timeout so the TOTAL stays ~BULK_TIMEOUT (+ a small connect)
356	    # and aligns with the outer headroom. Fall back to the float if httpx is absent.
357	    try:
358	        import httpx
359	
360	        _bulk_timeout: Any = httpx.Timeout(
361	            BULK_TIMEOUT, connect=min(10.0, BULK_TIMEOUT), pool=min(10.0, BULK_TIMEOUT)
362	        )
363	    except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - httpx ships with the reasoning extra
364	        _bulk_timeout = BULK_TIMEOUT
365	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = dict(
366	        model=model,
367	        base_url=BULK_BASE_URL,
368	        temperature=temperature,
369	        num_predict=BULK_NUM_PREDICT,  # bound generation length (H1)
370	        client_kwargs={"timeout": _bulk_timeout},
371	    )
372	    if seed is not None:
373	        kwargs["seed"] = seed
374	    if json_schema is not None:
375	        kwargs["format"] = json_schema  # Ollama schema-constrained decoding (reliable JSON)
376	    elif json_mode:
377	        kwargs["format"] = "json"  # loose native JSON decoding (syntactic only)
378	    client = ChatOllama(**kwargs)
379	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
380	    if system:
381	        messages.append(("system", system))
382	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
383	    try:
384	        reply = client.invoke(messages)
385	    except Exception as exc:
386	        # Prefer the typed HTTP status (ollama.ResponseError / httpx carry one): 404 = the model
387	        # isn't pulled → permanent. Fall back to substring matching only if no status is exposed
388	        # (robust to upstream message-wording changes — audit M4).
389	        status = getattr(exc, "status_code", None) or getattr(
390	            getattr(exc, "response", None), "status_code", None
391	        )
392	        msg = str(exc).lower()
393	        permanent = status == 404 or any(
394	            s in msg for s in ("not found", "no such model", "try pulling")
395	        )
396	        if permanent:
397	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"bulk model {model!r} not available: {exc}") from exc
398	        raise ReasoningError(f"bulk tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
399	    content = getattr(reply, "content", reply)
400	    text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
401	    text = text.strip()
402	    if not text:  # an empty reply is not a valid result — retry, don't return "" (issue #5/R5)
403	        raise ReasoningError("bulk tier returned an empty reply")
404	    return text
405	
406	
407	def _high_stakes_auth_present() -> bool:
408	    """Subscription auth available: an exported OAuth token OR a stored CLI credentials file
409	    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""
410	    if os.environ.get(OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV):
411	        return True
412	    return (Path.home() / ".claude" / ".credentials.json").is_file()
413	
414	
415	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
416	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
417	    try:
418	        import anyio
419	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
420	            AssistantMessage,
421	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
422	            ResultMessage,
423	            TextBlock,
424	            query,
425	        )
426	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
427	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
428	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
429	        ) from exc
430	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
431	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
432	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
433	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
434	        )
435	
436	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
437	        system_prompt=system,
438	        model=model,
439	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
440	        setting_sources=[],
441	        allowed_tools=[],
442	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
443	    )
444	
445	    parts: list[str] = []
446	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
447	
448	    async def _drain() -> None:
449	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
450	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
451	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
452	        #
453	        # Inner cancellation (#127): wrap the stream in anyio.fail_after so a hung subprocess is
454	        # actually CANCELLED (the cancellation propagates into query() and tears down the child),
455	        # freeing the bounded-pool worker. The outer _with_timeout only raises in the caller; it
456	        # cannot cancel this thread, so without this a hung HIGH_STAKES call would leak its slot
457	        # permanently. Fires before the outer wall-clock (HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30).
458	        nonlocal result_error
459	        with anyio.fail_after(HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT):
460	            await _drain_stream()
461	
462	    async def _drain_stream() -> None:
463	        nonlocal result_error
464	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
465	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
466	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
467	                if rl == "rejected":
468	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
469	                continue
470	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
471	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
472	                continue
473	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
474	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
475	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
476	                        parts.append(block.text)
477	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
478	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
479	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
480	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
481	                ):
482	                    result_error = {
483	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
484	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
485	                    }
486	
487	    try:
488	        anyio.run(_drain)
489	    except Exception as exc:
490	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
491	
492	    if result_error is not None:
493	        status = result_error.get("status")
494	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
495	        if status in (429, 529):
496	            raise RateLimitError(f"high-stakes tier rate window ({status}): {detail}")
497	        if status in (401, 403):
498	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier auth failed ({status}): {detail}")
499	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
500	
501	    text = "".join(parts).strip()
502	    if not text:  # empty frontier reply is not a valid result — retry (issue #R5)
503	        raise ReasoningError("high-stakes tier returned an empty reply")
504	    return text
505	
506	
507	def _attempt(request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
508	    """Route one request to its tier backend (no retry here)."""
509	    if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
510	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning is disabled (ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING)")
511	    p = request.params
512	    system = p.get("system")
513	    model = p.get("model")
514	    json_mode = bool(p.get("_json_mode", False))
515	    json_schema = p.get("json_schema")  # optional caller schema → constrained JSON decoding
516	    # Coerce numeric params behind the typed error contract (#H-004): a malformed temperature/seed
517	    # (e.g. "hot") would otherwise raise a raw ValueError/TypeError that escapes run()/run_json,
518	    # which document only ReasoningError. A bad param is a caller bug, not transient → permanent.
519	    try:
520	        temperature = float(p.get("temperature", 0.0))
521	        seed_raw = p.get("_seed")
522	        seed = int(seed_raw) if seed_raw is not None else None
523	    except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
524	        raise PermanentReasoningError(f"malformed reasoning params: {exc}") from exc
525	    if request.tier is StakesTier.BULK:
526	        return cast(
527	            str,
528	            _with_timeout(
529	                lambda: _bulk_complete(
530	                    request.prompt,
531	                    system,
532	                    temperature,
533	                    model or BULK_MODEL,
534	                    json_mode,
535	                    seed=seed,  # already coerced to int|None above (#H-004)
536	                    json_schema=json_schema,
537	                ),
538	                BULK_TIMEOUT + 10,
539	                "bulk tier",
540	            ),
541	        )
542	    if request.tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
543	        # The high-stakes tier runs the Claude CLI subprocess (not the in-process Anthropic SDK,
544	        # so no auto-instrumentor covers it) — emit an explicit OpenInference LLM span so Phoenix
545	        # records the model/provider for this otherwise-untraced tier (#2).
546	        llm_attrs = {
547	            "llm.provider": "anthropic",
548	            "llm.system": "anthropic",
549	            "llm.model_name": str(model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL or "subscription-default"),
550	        }
551	        with _span("reasoning.high_stakes", llm_attrs, kind="LLM"):
552	            return cast(
553	                str,
554	                _with_timeout(
555	                    lambda: _high_stakes_complete(
556	                        request.prompt, system, model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL
557	                    ),
558	                    HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30,
559	                    "high-stakes tier",
560	                ),
561	            )
562	    raise PermanentReasoningError(f"unknown tier: {request.tier!r}")
563	
564	
565	def _sleep_bounded(secs: float, deadline: float | None) -> None:
566	    """Sleep ``secs``, but never past ``deadline`` — so backoff can't blow an overall budget.
567	
568	    If the deadline has already passed, raise instead of sleeping the full interval, so a long
569	    rate-limit backoff cannot keep a caller blocked well beyond its deadline (#102).
570	    """
571	    if deadline is not None:
572	        remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()
573	        if remaining <= 0:
574	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded during backoff")
575	        secs = min(secs, remaining)
576	    time.sleep(secs)
577	
578	
579	def _run_with_retries(request: ReasoningRequest, *, deadline: float | None = None) -> str:
580	    """Execute with independent transient and rate-limit retry budgets.
581	
582	    ``deadline`` (monotonic seconds) bounds both the cumulative backoff AND entry into each
583	    retry: once the deadline has passed, no further ``_attempt`` is started (#102, #F-022). It
584	    does not interrupt an attempt already in flight — that is bounded by the per-call timeout —
585	    so total wall-clock is at most ``deadline`` plus one in-flight attempt's timeout, not
586	    ``deadline`` plus the full retry budget of per-call timeouts.
587	    """
588	    transient = 0
589	    rate = 0
590	    attempted = False
591	    while True:
592	        # Gate retry ENTRY on the deadline (#F-022): the first attempt always runs, but a retry is
593	        # not started once the deadline is past — otherwise the per-attempt timeouts could stack
594	        # well beyond the overall budget even though the backoff sleeps were bounded.
595	        if attempted and deadline is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
596	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded before retry")
597	        attempted = True
598	        try:
599	            return _attempt(request)
600	        except PermanentReasoningError:
601	            raise
602	        except RateLimitError:
603	            if rate >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
604	                raise
605	            _sleep_bounded(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate + 1), deadline)
606	            rate += 1
607	        except ReasoningError:
608	            if transient >= MAX_RETRIES:
609	                raise
610	            _sleep_bounded(BACKOFF_BASE**transient, deadline)
611	            transient += 1
612	
613	
614	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
615	# Public seam
616	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
617	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
618	class ReasoningSeam(Protocol):
619	    """Route a reasoning request to the appropriate tier with timeouts, retries, tracing."""
620	
621	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str: ...
622	
623	
624	@contextmanager
625	def _span(name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any], *, kind: str = "AGENT") -> Iterator[None]:
626	    """Emit an OpenTelemetry span if a provider is registered; a cheap no-op otherwise.
627	
628	    Stamps the OpenInference ``openinference.span.kind`` (default ``AGENT`` — a reasoning call)
629	    so Phoenix classifies the span instead of rendering it UNKNOWN. OpenTelemetry is imported
630	    lazily, so the seam traces when the (self-hosted, on-box) observability stack is wired and is
631	    a silent no-op in tests / offline (ADR-0003).
632	    """
633	    try:
634	        from opentelemetry import trace
635	    except ImportError:
636	        yield
637	        return
638	    tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine.reasoning")
639	    # start_as_current_span auto-records exceptions + sets ERROR status by default (#F-005 was a
640	    # false positive — see assay_engine.observability.tracing for the verification note).
641	    with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as span:
642	        span.set_attribute("openinference.span.kind", kind)
643	        for k, v in attributes.items():
644	            span.set_attribute(k, v)
645	        yield
646	
647	
648	class TieredReasoningSeam:
649	    """Concrete tiered seam. ``run`` returns text; ``run_json`` parses a JSON reply.
650	
651	    Backends and tracing are resolved lazily, so an instance constructs with no external
652	    dependencies; calls fail loud (``PermanentReasoningError``) if a tier's backend or its
653	    subscription auth is absent. When the observability stack is present, each call emits an
654	    OpenTelemetry span; otherwise tracing is a no-op.
655	    """
656	
657	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
658	        attrs = {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose}
659	        with _span("reasoning.run", attrs):
660	            return _run_with_retries(request)
661	
662	    def run_json(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> Any:
663	        """Generate and parse JSON, re-rolling on parse failure.
664	
665	        If the caller supplies ``params["json_schema"]``, the BULK tier uses Ollama's
666	        schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path). Otherwise it falls back to loose JSON
667	        mode + prompt instruction. BULK decoding can be deterministic (temp 0), so re-issuing the
668	        identical prompt would yield a byte-identical bad reply; each retry **varies the seed**
669	        (forcing a different deterministic decode) and *monotonically* raises temperature toward
670	        1.0 — never lowering it, so re-rolls never sharpen back toward the same output. ``params``
671	        is frozen, so each attempt builds a fresh request (issue #R6).
672	        """
673	        base_temp = float(request.params.get("temperature", 0.0))
674	        system = request.params.get("system")
675	        json_system = (
676	            f"{system}\n\nRespond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
677	            if system
678	            else "Respond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
679	        )
680	        last: ReasoningError | None = None
681	        deadline = time.monotonic() + RUN_JSON_DEADLINE  # overall bound across all re-rolls (#102)
682	        with _span(
683	            "reasoning.run_json",
684	            {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose},
685	        ):
686	            for attempt in range(JSON_REROLLS + 1):
687	                if attempt > 0 and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
688	                    raise last or ReasoningError("run_json exceeded its overall deadline")
689	                temp = min(1.0, base_temp + 0.2 * attempt)  # monotonic, never decreases (M3)
690	                params = dict(request.params)
691	                params.update(temperature=temp, system=json_system, _json_mode=True, _seed=attempt)
692	                attempt_req = ReasoningRequest(
693	                    prompt=request.prompt,
694	                    tier=request.tier,
695	                    purpose=request.purpose,
696	                    params=params,
697	                )
698	                # A transient/rate/permanent error is already handled (and bounded by the shared
699	                # deadline) inside _run_with_retries — let it PROPAGATE rather than re-rolling, so
700	                # the budgets do NOT multiply (#102). We re-roll ONLY on a JSON *parse* failure.
701	                text = _run_with_retries(attempt_req, deadline=deadline)
702	                try:
703	                    return extract_json(text)
704	                except ReasoningError as exc:  # parse failure → re-roll with a new seed
705	                    last = exc
706	            raise last if last is not None else ReasoningError("run_json failed")
707	
708	    @staticmethod
709	    def is_available(tier: StakesTier) -> bool:
710	        """Cheap liveness check (spends no LLM turn). Never raises — returns False on error."""
711	        if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
712	            return False
713	        try:
714	            if tier is StakesTier.BULK:
715	                require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
716	                import urllib.request
717	
718	                with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{BULK_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=2) as resp:
719	                    return bool(resp.status == 200)
720	            if tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
721	                import shutil
722	
723	                return shutil.which("claude") is not None and _high_stakes_auth_present()
724	        except Exception:
725	            return False
726	        return False
727	
728	
729	class UnconfiguredReasoningSeam:
730	    """Explicit fail-loud seam for contexts that must not perform reasoning."""
731	
732	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
733	        raise NotImplementedError(
734	            "this context uses an UnconfiguredReasoningSeam — supply a TieredReasoningSeam "
735	            "(or a test double) to perform reasoning"
736	        )

The `_inflight` accounting and `_release` idempotency, plus a subtle issue: `_submit_bounded` increments `_inflight` then submits. 1	"""Durable run-state checkpointer — local, data-sovereign (ADR-0003).
2	
3	The orchestration graph persists its full state (current phase, gate decisions, locked
4	hypotheses, verdicts) through a checkpointer so a run can interrupt at a human-approval gate
5	and resume later. That state holds the entire analysis, so the store is **on-box only**
6	(ADR-0003); a SaaS checkpointer is disqualified.
7	
8	This module is the LangGraph checkpointer factory, ported faithfully from the prior
9	platform's hardened implementation and generalized (dataset-agnostic ``ASSAY_*`` env, no
10	config-file coupling — see ADR-0006). The hardened behaviors are deliberately preserved
11	because each prevents a real production failure:
12	
13	- a **self-healing ConnectionPool** (not a raw connection): when Postgres goes away — node
14	  sleep, restart, idle timeout, a network blip — a cached raw connection is permanently
15	  broken and every later checkpoint op raises, i.e. durability is lost exactly during the
16	  interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to support. The pool runs a liveness check on
17	  checkout and replaces dead connections;
18	- a **session advisory lock** serializing first-time schema setup across *processes*, run on
19	  the *same* connection as the DDL (a pool would otherwise check out a different connection,
20	  leaving the lock ineffective);
21	- a **once-per-process-per-conn guard** (``setup()`` is idempotent but re-locking + replaying
22	  the migration query on every call is wasteful), with an in-process lock making the
23	  check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup;
24	- **credential redaction**: a backend exception (psycopg/psycopg_pool) can embed the full
25	  ``user:password`` URI/DSN in its own message, so the conn string is scrubbed precisely and
26	  the exception text via URI-userinfo + DSN-password backstops; the redacted message is then
27	  raised *outside* the exception handler, so the credential-bearing original is retained as
28	  neither ``__cause__`` nor ``__context__`` (a logger walking ``__context__`` cannot
29	  re-expose it);
30	- a single shared **atexit** pool-cleanup handler (one per process, not one per call) so the
31	  pool's worker thread is joined before interpreter finalization.
32	
33	LangGraph/psycopg are optional (the ``persistence`` extra) and imported lazily; the
34	credential-redaction and connection-resolution logic is pure and unit-tested offline.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import atexit
40	import os
41	import re
42	import threading
43	from typing import Any, cast
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	from assay_engine._envparse import int_env
47	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
48	
49	_DEFAULT_PG = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
50	
51	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes checkpoint
52	# schema migrations across concurrent initializers. The value is irrelevant beyond being a
53	# stable per-logical-schema constant; "ASSY" as an int.
54	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4153_5359
55	
56	# Upper bound (milliseconds) on how long the cross-process advisory lock may be waited for
57	# during schema bootstrap. A stuck/crashed peer that still holds a live PG session on the lock
58	# must NOT be able to hang bootstrap forever (#129); lock_timeout aborts the wait. 0 disables
59	# the bound (block indefinitely, the pre-#129 behavior). Read fresh on each bootstrap (#F-033) so
60	# an env var set after import still takes effect; the module constant is the default + the value
61	# tests monkeypatch.
62	_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS = max(0, int_env("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS", "30000"))
63	
64	
65	def _migration_lock_timeout_ms() -> int:
66	    """The advisory-lock wait bound, re-read from the env each call (#F-033).
67	
68	    Falls back to the import-time module constant when the env var is unset, so a test that
69	    monkeypatches ``_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS`` still drives the value.
70	    """
71	    raw = os.environ.get("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS")
72	    if raw is None or raw == "":
73	        return _MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS
74	    return max(0, int_env("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS", "30000"))  # names the var on error
75	
76	
77	# Brief meta-lock guarding the process-local registries below (atexit registration, the open-pool
78	# list, the per-conn lock map, the pool cache). It is held ONLY for in-memory check-and-set — it
79	# is NEVER held across blocking cross-process I/O (pool.connection(), the advisory-lock wait, or
80	# setup() DDL). That blocking work is serialized per conn_str instead (#129), so a stuck advisory
81	# lock on one database cannot block initialization of an unrelated one.
82	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
83	
84	# Per-conn_str bootstrap locks: only same-conn_str initializers serialize; different databases
85	# bootstrap concurrently. Created lazily under _init_lock.
86	_CONN_INIT_LOCKS: dict[str, threading.Lock] = {}
87	
88	# Live pools cached by conn_str so the factory reuses one self-healing pool (and its worker
89	# thread) per database rather than opening a fresh pool on every call (#144).
90	_POOLS_BY_CONN: dict[str, Any] = {}
91	
92	# Pools opened by the factory, closed once at process exit via a single shared atexit handler
93	# (registering one handler per call would accumulate handlers across repeated factory calls).
94	_OPEN_POOLS: list[Any] = []
95	_atexit_registered = False
96	
97	# Strips ``://user:password@`` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. '/' is allowed
98	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the userinfo
99	# terminator) and whitespace bound the match.
100	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[^@\s]+@")
101	# Strips a libpq keyword/value DSN password token (``password=...`` / ``pgpassword=...``),
102	# handling quoted values that may contain spaces.
103	_DSN_PW_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b(password|pgpassword)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
104	
105	
106	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
107	    """Return ``scheme://host[:port]/path`` with any ``user:password`` userinfo stripped.
108	
109	    Robust to malformed userinfo: a password containing ``/``/``#`` makes ``urlsplit``'s
110	    ``.port`` raise ``ValueError`` on access, so the whole parse (including attribute access)
111	    is guarded and falls back to the generic userinfo-strip regex / a constant placeholder.
112	    """
113	    try:
114	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
115	        host = u.hostname or ""
116	        port = u.port
117	        scheme = u.scheme
118	        path = u.path
119	    except ValueError:
120	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str)
121	    if not host:
122	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str) if scheme else "<store>"
123	    if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
124	        host = f"[{host}]"
125	    netloc = f"{host}:{port}" if port else host
126	    return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}" if scheme else "<store>"
127	
128	
129	def redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
130	    """Remove credentials a backend exception may embed in its message text.
131	
132	    Layers, in order of precision:
133	
134	    1. exact replacement of the known ``conn_str`` (handles any password characters, incl.
135	       ``/`` and spaces) — this is the precise layer and covers the real connection string;
136	    2. a generic URI-userinfo strip (``://user:pass@`` → ``://``) as a backstop for any
137	       *other* URI in the text;
138	    3. a libpq DSN ``password=``/``pgpassword=`` strip for the keyword/value form.
139	
140	    Layers 2–3 are best-effort for *secondary* endpoints (e.g. a replica DSN) the caller did
141	    not pass as ``conn_str``.
142	
143	    Precision caveat (pass 5, #H-023): layer 1 is an EXACT-substring replacement, so it scrubs the
144	    primary connection string precisely only when that string appears verbatim in ``text``. If a
145	    backend reformats it (re-quoting, reordering DSN keywords, percent-encoding the password) the
146	    verbatim match misses and only the layers-2–3 regex backstops apply — which still strip
147	    ``://user:pass@`` userinfo and ``password=`` tokens, but are not guaranteed exhaustive for
148	    every backend message shape. It is "precise when the conn string appears verbatim", not
149	    "always precise".
150	    """
151	    if conn_str:
152	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
153	    text = _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
154	    return _DSN_PW_RE.sub(r"\1=***", text)
155	
156	
157	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
158	    """Resolve the Postgres connection string (``ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`` env → loopback default).
159	
160	    The env path bypasses any external config validation, so loopback is enforced here at the
161	    point of use (ADR-0003 defense-in-depth): graph state must not leave the box.
162	    """
163	    url = os.environ.get("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL") or _DEFAULT_PG
164	    return require_local_uri(url, what="checkpointer connection")
165	
166	
167	def _safe_close(pool: Any) -> None:
168	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing ``close()`` does not abort
169	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (``close()`` calls a C ``finish()``
170	    that can raise)."""
171	    try:
172	        pool.close()
173	    except Exception:
174	        pass
175	
176	
177	def _close_all_pools() -> None:
178	    # Snapshot under the meta-lock before iterating (#F-020): _OPEN_POOLS is appended under
179	    # _init_lock elsewhere, and on a free-threaded (GIL-less) build a concurrent list.append
180	    # reallocation during iteration is undefined behavior. _safe_close never takes _init_lock, so
181	    # closing outside the lock cannot deadlock.
182	    with _init_lock:
183	        pools = list(_OPEN_POOLS)
184	    for p in pools:
185	        _safe_close(p)
186	
187	
188	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool: Any) -> None:
189	    global _atexit_registered
190	    with _init_lock:
191	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
192	        if not _atexit_registered:
193	            atexit.register(_close_all_pools)
194	            _atexit_registered = True
195	
196	
197	def _conn_init_lock(conn_str: str) -> threading.Lock:
198	    """Return the per-conn_str bootstrap lock, creating it under the brief meta-lock (#129)."""
199	    with _init_lock:
200	        lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS.get(conn_str)
201	        if lock is None:
202	            lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS[conn_str] = threading.Lock()
203	        return lock
204	
205	
206	def _cached_pool(conn_str: str) -> Any:
207	    with _init_lock:
208	        return _POOLS_BY_CONN.get(conn_str)
209	
210	
211	def _acquire_migration_lock(conn: Any) -> None:
212	    """Acquire the cross-process schema-migration advisory lock with a bounded wait (#129).
213	
214	    ``lock_timeout`` aborts the ``pg_advisory_lock`` wait if a peer holds it past the deadline,
215	    so a stuck/crashed initializer cannot hang bootstrap indefinitely; the resulting error
216	    propagates as a redacted ``RuntimeError`` like any other backend failure. set_config is used
217	    (not ``SET``) so the value can be safely parameterized.
218	    """
219	    timeout_ms = _migration_lock_timeout_ms()
220	    if timeout_ms:
221	        conn.execute("SELECT set_config('lock_timeout', %s, false)", (str(timeout_ms),))
222	    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
223	
224	
225	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
226	    """Return a configured LangGraph checkpointer.
227	
228	    ``use_memory=True`` returns an in-memory saver (tests / no-Postgres). Otherwise a
229	    Postgres-backed saver over a self-healing pool, with advisory-locked one-time schema
230	    setup. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (with credentials redacted) if the backend is unreachable
231	    or the extra is not installed.
232	    """
233	    try:
234	        if use_memory:
235	            from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
236	
237	            return MemorySaver()
238	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
239	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
240	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
241	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
242	        raise RuntimeError(
243	            "Postgres checkpointer requires the 'persistence' extra "
244	            "(langgraph-checkpoint-postgres, psycopg[binary,pool]) — not installed"
245	        ) from exc
246	
247	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
248	
249	    # Fast path: reuse a live, self-healing pool for this conn_str rather than opening a new pool
250	    # (and its background worker thread) on every call (#144). The liveness check on checkout
251	    # replaces dead connections, so a cached pool stays valid across transient outages.
252	    cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)
253	    if cached is not None:
254	        return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
255	
256	    # Serialize bootstrap PER conn_str — NOT process-globally (#129). Unrelated databases
257	    # initialize concurrently, and a stuck advisory lock on one conn_str can never block init of
258	    # another. The brief meta-lock (_init_lock) is only taken inside the helpers, never held
259	    # across the blocking advisory-lock wait / DDL below.
260	    with _conn_init_lock(conn_str):
261	        cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)  # another thread may have built it while we waited
262	        if cached is not None:
263	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
264	
265	        failure: str | None = None
266	        pool: Any = None
267	        try:
268	            # Pool bounds are env-tunable (#F-040): the reasoning seam already runs 8 worker
269	            # threads, so a fixed max_size=8 can be saturated under concurrent reasoning+checkpoint
270	            # load on larger hardware. connect_timeout bounds an individual TCP connect so a
271	            # black-holed host fails fast instead of hanging bootstrap for the OS default.
272	            min_size = max(0, int_env("ASSAY_POOL_MIN_SIZE", "1"))
273	            max_size = max(min_size or 1, int_env("ASSAY_POOL_MAX_SIZE", "8"))
274	            connect_timeout = max(1, int_env("ASSAY_POOL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT", "10"))
275	            pool = ConnectionPool(
276	                conn_str,
277	                min_size=min_size,
278	                max_size=max_size,
279	                kwargs={
280	                    "autocommit": True,
281	                    "prepare_threshold": 0,
282	                    "row_factory": dict_row,
283	                    "connect_timeout": connect_timeout,
284	                },
285	                check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,  # liveness check on checkout (self-healing)
286	                open=True,
287	            )
288	            _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
289	            # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. The advisory lock and the DDL
290	            # must run on the SAME connection (pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped); a pool-backed
291	            # setup() would lock one connection and DDL on another, leaving the lock ineffective.
292	            with pool.connection() as conn:
293	                _acquire_migration_lock(conn)  # bounded wait so a stuck peer can't hang us (#129)
294	                try:
295	                    # row_factory=dict_row makes this connection dict-rowed at runtime
296	                    # (what PostgresSaver requires); the cast asserts that runtime truth
297	                    # the type system can't infer from the pool kwargs.
298	                    PostgresSaver(cast(Any, conn)).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
299	                finally:
300	                    # Best-effort unlock; the lock auto-releases when the connection
301	                    # returns to the pool, and raising here would mask a setup() error.
302	                    try:
303	                        conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
304	                    except Exception:
305	                        pass
306	                    # Reset the session lock_timeout GUC (#G-002): the autocommit connection set it
307	                    # at SESSION scope for the bootstrap, then returns to the pool and is reused for
308	                    # all later checkpoint reads/writes — which would otherwise silently inherit a
309	                    # non-default lock_timeout the operator never configured for normal ops.
310	                    try:
311	                        conn.execute("RESET lock_timeout")
312	                    except Exception:
313	                        pass
314	            with _init_lock:
315	                _POOLS_BY_CONN[conn_str] = pool  # cache for reuse on later calls (#144)
316	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, pool))  # dict-rowed pool (row_factory=dict_row)
317	        except Exception as exc:
318	            # Build the redacted message here, but raise it OUTSIDE the handler (below) so the
319	            # original — credential-bearing — exception is retained as neither __cause__ NOR
320	            # __context__ (a logger walking __context__ would otherwise re-expose the password).
321	            failure = (
322	                f"Postgres checkpointer connection failed (is Postgres running on "
323	                f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
324	                "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
325	            )
326	            # The pool may have opened before bootstrap failed — close it and drop it from the
327	            # cleanup registry so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread (#105).
328	            if pool is not None:
329	                _safe_close(pool)
330	                with _init_lock:
331	                    if pool in _OPEN_POOLS:
332	                        _OPEN_POOLS.remove(pool)
333	        raise RuntimeError(failure)
334	
335	
336	def configured_checkpointer(use_memory: bool | None = None) -> Any:
337	    """Return the checkpointer the environment selects.
338	
339	    ``use_memory`` (or ``ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND=memory``) chooses the in-memory saver;
340	    otherwise Postgres.
341	    """
342	    if use_memory is None:
343	        use_memory = os.environ.get("ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND", "postgres").lower() == "memory"
344	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)

1	"""Experiment tracking — a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        # check-then-create is a TOCTOU (#J-004): two concurrent first-time start_run calls (the
69	        # 8-thread reasoning pool, or multiple study processes on one shared local store) can both
70	        # see None and both create_experiment; MLflow rejects the loser with RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS
71	        # and the exception would propagate out of start_run, leaving run_id=None and silently
72	        # dropping ALL metrics + the provenance artifact for the whole study. On a create race,
73	        # re-fetch the now-existing experiment instead of failing.
74	        try:
75	            return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
76	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - normalize the concurrent-create race to a re-fetch
77	            exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
78	            if exp is not None:
79	                return str(exp.experiment_id)
80	            raise
81	
82	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
83	        client = self._client()
84	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
85	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
86	        # The run now exists in the store. If param-logging fails partway (network blip, server
87	        # error), the run would otherwise be orphaned permanently in RUNNING state — phantom runs
88	        # accumulate over time (pass 3, #F-023). Terminate it FAILED on any param-logging error
89	        # before re-raising, so a created run is never left dangling.
90	        try:
91	            for k, v in dict(params).items():
92	                client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
93	        except Exception:
94	            try:
95	                client.set_terminated(run_id, status="FAILED")
96	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 - best-effort cleanup; original error re-raised
97	                pass
98	            raise
99	        return run_id
100	
101	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
102	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
103	
104	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
105	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
106	
107	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None:
108	        # MLflow run statuses: FINISHED / FAILED / KILLED. Recording FAILED makes a failed run
109	        # distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110).
110	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id, status=status)
111

Vectorstore tracks ownership and closes. Now let me dig into the most promising concrete bug. Let me re-examine the `_run_with_retries` deadline logic for the rate-limit retry counter vs the saturation `RateLimitError`.

When the pool is saturated, `_submit_bounded` raises `RateLimitError`. In `_run_with_retries`, this is caught by `except RateLimitError`, sleeps `RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate+1)` (default 30s), and retries up to `RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES` (5). So a saturated pool causes a caller to wait 30+60+90+120+150 = 450s before failing. That's a documented backpressure design.

But here's a real concern: **saturation `RateLimitError` and actual API rate-limit `RateLimitError` share the same retry budget**. If pool is saturated due to genuinely hung slots (timed-out backends still holding slots), then EVERY caller will spin through 5 rate-limit retries with long sleeps. Since timed-out slots are never freed until the backend returns, and the saturation guard's whole point is to fail fast — but it actually makes callers sleep 450s. That's arguably by-design backpressure though.

No parallelism in phases — phases run sequentially. The only thread pool is the reasoning timeout pool. 

Let me reconsider the timeout pool's most serious reliability property: **timed-out slots are never reclaimed until the backend returns**. For BULK, the inner httpx timeout frees the slot. For HIGH_STAKES, the inner `anyio.fail_after` is supposed to. But there's a real gap: the `_attempt` for HIGH_STAKES wraps `_high_stakes_complete` (which calls `anyio.run(_drain)`) in `_with_timeout(..., HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30)`. The inner `anyio.fail_after(HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT)` is INSIDE `_drain`. So inner fires at `HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT`, outer at `+30`. If `anyio.fail_after` raises `TimeoutError` inside `_drain`, that propagates through `anyio.run` → caught by `except Exception as exc` → raised as `ReasoningError`. The worker thread returns, callback fires, slot freed. Good — but only IF the cancellation actually unwinds. If `query()` ignores cancellation (blocked in a non-cancellable subprocess read), `anyio.fail_after` can't force it, and the worker blocks until the outer raises in the caller — but the slot stays held (outer `_with_timeout` doesn't cancel the worker). This is the documented "slot held until backend returns" — but for HIGH_STAKES there's NO equivalent of httpx's hard socket timeout on the subprocess. So a hung claude subprocess leaks the slot indefinitely. 

This is a real, concrete reliability gap, but it depends on the SDK's cancellation behavior, which I can't fully falsify here. It's also arguably mitigated by the saturation guard. I'll note it as medium with honest framing.

Let me check one more concrete thing — whether `anyio.fail_after` is even the right primitive and whether `_drain`'s structure handles the cancellation correctly. Let me re-read: `_drain` does `with anyio.fail_after(...)`. The cancellation in anyio is checkpoint-based; `query()` is an async generator that should yield at await points. Reasonable. 

Let me now look at a likely-real bug: in `run_json`, the per-attempt request sets `_seed=attempt` (0,1,2). But for the **HIGH_STAKES tier, seed is ignored** (only BULK uses seed). So for high-stakes, re-rolls on parse failure re-issue with monotonically rising temperature but the same prompt — fine, temperature varies. OK.

Let me verify the deadline interaction: `RUN_JSON_DEADLINE` default 600s, but `_run_with_retries` gets `deadline=deadline`. Within it, rate-limit backoff sleeps are bounded by deadline. But the **first attempt of each re-roll always runs** regardless of remaining time (only retry ENTRY is gated). And `run_json`'s loop checks deadline at top for attempt>0. So worst case: attempt exhausts near deadline, returns parse failure, loop checks deadline, raises. Bounded. Good.

content.text:
The SDK has a `__aexit__`/teardown that terminates the subprocess after a graceful shutdown timeout. But the critical question: when `anyio.fail_after` cancels mid-iteration, does the `async for` loop's cleanup (`query()`'s context manager `__aexit__`) actually run to terminate the subprocess? The SDK does terminate on `disconnect`. But the cancellation needs to propagate through `query()` which is an async generator — anyio's `fail_after` raises into the generator at the next checkpoint, and the generator's `finally`/`__aexit__` should run the disconnect. So slot freeing depends on graceful teardown completing within the SDK's own internal timeouts. This is reasonable and the inner `fail_after` is the right mechanism.

The `prior is None` case (a SIGTERM handler installed by non-Python/C code, where `signal.getsignal` documents it returns `None`) falls through both branches and silently swallows SIGTERM — the exact hang the comment claims to prevent. findings.title: SIGTERM handler swallows the signal when a prior C-installed handler makes getsignal() return None
findings.claim: _handler captures prior = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM) at install time and, on SIGTERM, chains only if callable(prior) or re-delivers the default only if prior == signal.SIG_DFL. signal.getsignal() is documented to return None when the SIGTERM handler was installed from non-Python (C) code. In that case prior is None: neither branch runs, _handler flushes and RETURNS, and because signal.signal replaced SIGTERM's default terminate action with this flush-and-return, the process does NOT terminate on SIGTERM. docker stop / kill / k8s then hang until SIGKILL escalation (~10s) — the exact hang the code comment (#K-REL-1) claims to prevent. The comment enumerates SIG_DFL and SIG_IGN as the only non-callable cases ('SIG_IGN stays ignored') and omits None.
findings.why_real: Verified the code path by reading lines 159-163: the if/elif has no else and does not cover prior is None. Python's signal.getsignal docs explicitly state it returns None if the handler was not installed from Python. The failure mode (SIGTERM swallowed -> container/orchestrator stop hangs until SIGKILL) is concrete and matches the documented hazard the branch was written to avoid; it is simply not covered for the None disposition. The test suite (test_observability.py) only exercises the callable and SIG_DFL cases.
findings.suggested_fix: Treat None (and any non-callable, non-SIG_IGN disposition) the same as SIG_DFL: change `elif prior == signal.SIG_DFL:` to also re-establish the default and re-deliver when prior is None, e.g. `elif prior in (signal.SIG_DFL, None):`. Add a regression test that monkeypatches signal.getsignal to return None and asserts the handler resets to SIG_DFL and calls os.kill (re-delivery), mirroring the existing SIG_DFL test.

content.text:
I completed an adversarial correctness/concurrency/reliability review of the hc-assay engine on branch `harden/pass-10`, focused on the reasoning thread pool, cancellation/timeout, retry/backoff, run_study and MLflow lifecycle, checkpoint pool, signal-handler re-entrancy, and provenance/registry locking.

The dimension is genuinely well-hardened after nine passes. I empirically verified (not just read) the key invariants:
- `_inflight` accounting and saturation guard return to 0 after drain (`seam.py`), with per-future idempotent `_release`.
- httpx Timeout phases bound total BULK wall-clock as the comment claims (write inherits the 120s positional default; connect/pool capped at 10s).
- The balanced-brace JSON walker handles escapes and trailing prose correctly.
- The SIGTERM re-delivery (pass-8 #K-REL-1) is correctly implemented and tested for callable and SIG_DFL prior dispositions.
- `anyio` is correctly declared in the `reasoning` extra and is a transitive SDK dep; the claude-agent-sdk does terminate child subprocesses on teardown, and the inner `anyio.fail_after` is the right cancellation primitive.
- 564 tests pass; main CI is green.

One genuinely concrete, falsifiable gap found (reported, low severity):

`src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py:159-163` — the SIGTERM `_handler` covers `callable(prior)` and `prior == signal.SIG_DFL`, but not the case where `signal.getsignal()` returns `None` (documented behavior when a prior handler was installed by C/non-Python code). In that case the handler flushes and returns without re-delivering the terminate action, swallowing SIGTERM and hanging `docker stop`/`kill`/k8s until SIGKILL escalation — the exact failure the #K-REL-1 comment claims to prevent. Fix: treat `None` like `SIG_DFL` (`elif prior in (signal.SIG_DFL, None):`) and add a `getsignal -> None` regression test.

Candidate gaps I deliberately did NOT report as defects (honest framing): the saturation `RateLimitError` sharing the rate-limit retry budget (intentional backpressure, no slot leak — caller-thread sleeps only), and the theoretical HIGH_STAKES hung-subprocess slot leak (mitigated by the correct inner `anyio.fail_after` cancellation plus the saturation guard; depends on SDK cancellation semantics that the code uses correctly).
